Haris Malik: Hey team, hi! I'm Haris — joining Developer Experience today. Excited to get started. Quick setup Qs: who do I ping for MDM enrollment and laptop VPN? Also who is my onboarding buddy? PS: allergic to peanuts, jealous of your sticker collections :grimacing:. Fun fact: I do midnight pottery and have a tiny collection of vintage train tickets.

onboard-bot: Welcome Haris :tada: — we've created your account. Please follow the checklist: https://redwood.internal/onboard/checklist and enroll in MDM here: https://redwood.internal/it/mdm. If you hit issues, reply here and tag @marta-it.

Ava Chen: Welcome Haris!! I'm your buddy for the first month — calendar invite sent for a 30m sync tomorrow 10am PT. I'll add you to #devex-buddies. For quick access, here's the VPN client config link: https://redwood.internal/it/vpn — follow step 2 for the new profile.

Lela Lane: Hey everyone! I'm Lela — joining today as a PM on Growth. First day in the SF office, desk by the big ficus. I bake pancakes and sourdough on weekends and I collect tiny succulents and old typewriters. Bringing a small pancake batch tomorrow if anyone wants some. Also curious: best lunchtime spots near 5th & Market? :)

Ava Chen (People Ops): Welcome Lela!! :tada: Pancakes = absolutely yes. Recommend 'Batter & Bloom' two blocks over — lemon ricotta is my go-to. Also we'll do a quick welcome lunch at 12:30 today, team will meet at the lobby.

Raj Patel: hey welcome! For good espresso try Caffè Duomo on 7th. We also have a low-key keyboard meetup on Wednesdays in the lounge — typewriters -> mechanical keyboards crossover is a thing here :grin:

Maya Chen: hi Lela — i'm on DevEx. Ping me if you want a quick console walkthrough or help with dotfiles/setup. Also FYI we run a com...

Olivia Hart: Hey team Olivia here (first week). Two quick setup asks so I can run e2e infra tests: 
- Link billing account `sandbox-billing-2026` to project `redwood-olivia-sbx` (tiny spend, < $30/mo)
- Need GKE access: roles/container.clusterViewer + roles/container.developer on cluster `redwood-dev-cluster` in europe-west4 and asia-south1 so I can deploy a test sidecar and run some latency experiments
- Also need `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser` on `sa-olivia@redwood-dev.iam.gserviceaccount.com` for CI to impersonate for deployments
- AWS cross-account read-only to account `999111222333` and S3 ListObjects on `redwood-model-checkpoints` to compare checkpoint sizes across regions
- Hitting Okta device-flow "invalid_grant" when doing `gcloud auth login` any quick fixes? Who should I tag for billing link + AWS role? Thanks!

Ethan Park: I did: `KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config:~/downloads/comp...
text:
Ethan Park: hey folks, first week here — trying to get kubectl set up. I followed the onboarding PDF and downloaded the company kubeconfig, but when I merge it with my personal file my kubectl commands hang then timeout. Anyone know what I should check?

Ethan Park: I did: `KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config:~/downloads/company-kube.yaml kubectl get nodes` and it just stalls then: `The connection to the server 10.9.3.5:6443 was refused - did you specify the right host or port?`

DevEx Bot: welcome Ethan! :wave: quick sanity list:
• confirm the active context with `kubectl config current-context`
• run `kubectl config view --minify -o jsonpath='{.clusters[0].cluster.server}'` to see which endpoint kubectl is trying to reach
• if you get an IP (10.x.x.x) that likely requires VPN or a bastion jump
• try `kubectl --kubeconfig=~/downloads/company-kube.yaml get nodes` to rule out merge problems

Eth...

text:
Maya Singh: Hi all I'm Maya, joining infra. Quick access checklist for first-week tests:
- Link billing account `billing-sandbox-2026` to project `sandbox-maya` (need to boot tiny VM in europe-west3)
- Grant me `roles/compute.viewer` and `roles/compute.instanceAdmin.v1` on project for sandbox testing
- GCP region access for europe-west3 and us-west1
- AWS cross-account read-only to acct 210987654321 and S3 ListObjects on `redwood-checkpoints`
Any pointers on who handles billing link and typical ETA?

Ethan Park: welcome! Ping @billing-team for account link. ETA usually same-day if you include project id and cost justification.

Maya Singh: justification added: "spin up sandbox VMs for infra onboarding; non-prod spend < $30/mo"

Lina Gomez: security note: use time-limited AWS role (default 72h). Do you need long-term SA keys? if not, we'll give serviceAccountUser only.

Maya Singh: no ...

Ethan Park: hey team, quick onboarding snag — Kandji shows 'Installing profiles' forever when I'm plugged into the USB-C dock. Switch to wifi and it finishes. macOS 14.5. Any tips?

Lena (IT): welcome Ethan :wave: do you see any error text? did you use the dock during first boot?

Ethan Park: just the spinner and 'Installing profiles'. yes docked at my desk for OOBE. unplugging finishes enrollment.

Kandji-bot: Enrollment attempt recorded for "Ethan Park - MacBookPro16" at 2026-10-12T09:04:04Z. Applied policy: "New Hire - MacOS".

Jordan (Device Admin): common pattern: some docks delay network initialization or create a virtual interface that confuses the enrollment agent. Kandji needs immediate HTTPS to our endpoints to escrow FV keys and apply scripts. Workarounds: 1) unplug dock, finish enrollment over wifi, then replug. 2) if you'd rather keep docked, open Terminal and run `sudo n...

text:
Aria Kim: Hey team — first day. Followed the onboarding steps but `kubectl config get-contexts` only shows 'default'. I need a personal dev namespace (aria-dev) to test previews. Who should I ping?

Ben Carter: welcome Aria :wave: Quick checks: did you complete Okta SSO and device enrollment? Run `redwood-sso login --okta` then `kubectl config view --minify --raw` and paste the contexts (sanitized).

Aria Kim: pasted sanitized output:
```
contexts:
- context:
 cluster: redwood-dev
 user: aria@redwood.ai
 name: redwood-dev
```

Ben Carter: that user looks like a raw email instead of the Okta exec user (`okta:aria@redwood.ai`). @oncall-k8s can you check whether Aria is in the Okta group that maps to k8s dev access (k8s-dev-access)?

oncall-k8s: checking... aria@redwood.ai is NOT a member of Okta group k8s-dev-access. Requesting group add.

infra-team-bot: Added aria@redwood.ai to Okta g...

text:
Nora Velasquez: hi everyone! Nora here, product design, first day. Two quick asks: 1) where's the short cheat-sheet for getting API creds and running a local mock service? 2) whats the 'two truths and a weird skill' tradition you all do? Also — fun fact: I can play the didgeridoo badly.

Jamie Lin: welcome @Nora Velasquez :tada: For creds, the one-pager is pinned in #devex and also in the onboarding doc in the wiki. Minimal flow: 1) 1Password invite -> request team/project entry, 2) request sandbox key (Devex Bot), 3) run the mock server docker image below.

Devex Bot: To spin up the local mock quickly:

```
docker run --rm -p 8080:8080 ghcr.io/redwood/mock-playground:stable
# then: http://localhost:8080/docs
```

Ben Carter: hi Nora! On the tradition: during the first-week intro call we each say two truths + one weird skill. No pressure but people love hearing about travel or hidden ...

text:
Ethan Park: hey all, day 2 and hitting a kube permissions issue. followed the kubeconfig guide but when I try `kubectl get pods -n feature-x` I get Forbidden. any quick triage tips? /cc @DevEx

DevEx: welcome Ethan! can you paste `kubectl config current-context` and the exact error? also are you on Linux or mac?

Ethan Park: current-context is `redwood-dev`; I'm on Ubuntu WSL. error: 
```
Error from server (Forbidden): pods is forbidden: User "user:ethan.park@redwood.com" cannot list resource "pods" in API group "" in the namespace "feature-x"
```
`kubectl auth can-i list pods -n feature-x` -> "no"

DevEx: two quick things: (1) check if your Okta groups include `redwood:eng-platform` or `redwood:team-feature-x` (we gate namespaces by team groups), and (2) check your kubeconfig for duplicate/merged users (common when you copy from laptop). run `kubectl config view --minify --flatten` t...

Girish Menon: Hi Kaan, welcome! Can you paste the exact error message? Most MDM failures are fixed by re-running the enrollment installer from our deploy box. URL: https://deploy.redwood.com/mdm — also make sure youre on the office VPN or connected to the guest network when doing enroll.

I think I also don't have billing linked. Who do I ping to get billing attached + the right IAM for networking?

Lina G...
text:
Jordan Park: Hey team — Jordan here, first day on infra. Trying to create a test VM in asia-southeast1 (project: redwood-analytics) and it fails with `PermissionDenied: compute.subnetworks.create`. I think I also don't have billing linked. Who do I ping to get billing attached + the right IAM for networking?

Lina Gomez: welcome Jordan! :wave: pinging infra folks. can you paste the exact error and the project id?

Jordan Park: error is `PermissionDenied: The caller does not have permission to perform compute.subnetworks.create on project redwood-analytics (or it may be missing BillingEnabled).` project id is `redwood-analytics` (same). quick repro: `gcloud compute networks subnets create test-subnet --network default --region asia-southeast1`

Raj Patel: two things likely: 1) billing not linked -> some APIs blocked, 2) missing IAM (roles/compute.networkAdmin or a narrower custom role)...

text:
Mina Okafor: Hi all first day! Set up my desk (B2-05) but when I plug into the dock my Mac loses Wi-Fi and the external keyboard stops responding. Laptop works fine on battery and builtin keyboard. Anyone seen this behavior?

Luis Romero: Welcome Mina! That's usually a dock power or firmware issue. Which dock model is on the sticker? Also are you on 'redwood-office' or 'redwood-guest'?

Mina Okafor: Sticker says 'PortBridge 4'. I'm on redwood-office. Ethernet through the dock works, but Wi-Fi drops and keyboard LED blinks then goes idle.

Ava Chen: Quick test: try connecting the keyboard directly to the Mac (not through the dock) and see if it works. Also try using the other USB-C cable from the ops cabinet if available.

Mina Okafor: Keyboard works when plugged directly. I swapped to a spare USB-C cable (from the box) and behavior is the same.

Luis Romero: Thanks. Creating ticket an...

title: #new-hires: 1725000000
preview:
Ellen Park: Hi all, I accepted the "Orientation: Nov 30" invite but my calendar shows it at 9am instead of 10am. I'm in Dublin. Is the invite wrong?
Mateo Alvarez: Hi @Ellen — did you get the regional repeat? We have both EMEA and Global invitations, sometimes both land in calendars. Which event title does it show e...
text:
Ellen Park: Hi all, I accepted the "Orientation: Nov 30" invite but my calendar shows it at 9am instead of 10am. I'm in Dublin. Is the invite wrong?
Mateo Alvarez: Hi @Ellen — did you get the regional repeat? We have both EMEA and Global invitations, sometimes both land in calendars. Which event title does it show exactly?
Ellen Park: It says 'Orientation: Redwood Welcome (Global)' and another says 'Orientation: Redwood Welcome (EMEA)'. The Global one is 09:00 and EMEA is 10:00. Which one should I follow?
Sana Rao: Keep the EMEA one — that's the canonical session for Dublin. We always recommend deleting the Global one to avoid duplicates.
Ellen Park: Got it. Any chance the ICS is using UTC and showing differently because DST just ended?
Mateo Alvarez: We specified times in UTC in the calendar send, but some clients render both UTC and local offsets. For Nov 30 the correct local time i...
